title: A Descriptive Poem on the Silvery Tay by William Topaz McGonagall
content:
Beautiful silvery Tay,
With your landscapes, so lovely and gay,
Along each side of your waters, to Perth all the way;
No other river in the world has

title: The Moon by William Topaz McGonagall
content:
Beautiful Moon, with thy silvery light,
Thou seemest most charming to my sight;
As I gaze upon thee in the sky so high,
A tear of joy does moisten min

title: A Tale of the Sea by William Topaz McGonagall
content:
A pathetic tale of the sea I will unfold,
Enough to make one's blood run cold;
Concerning four fishermen cast adrift in a dory.
As I've been told I'll

title: A Christmas Carol by William Topaz McGonagall
content:
Welcome, sweet Christmas, blest be the morn
That Christ our Saviour was born!
Earth's Redeemer, to save us from all danger,

title: The Inauguration of the Hill o' Balgay by William Topaz McGonagall
content:
Beautiful Hill o' Balgay,
With your green frees and flowers fair,
'Tis health for the old and young
For to be walking there,
